englishVocabulary.meaning: 提出, 擺出, 造成, 尋求
englishVocabulary.example: pose a problem, pose a threat, pose a challenge, pose a risk, pose a question
It is not easy to be systematic and objective about language study.
It is not easy to be systematic and objective about language study.
Popular linguistic debate regularly deteriorates into invective and polemic.
Popular linguistic debate regularly deteriorates into invective and polemic.
Language belongs to everyone, so most people feel they have a right to hold an opinion about it.
And when opinions differ, emotions can run high.
Arguments can start as easily over minor points of usage.
Arguments can start as easily over minor points of usage as over major policies of linguistic education.
major policies of linguistic education.
linguistic factors influence how we judge personality, intelligence, social status.
linguistic factors influence how we judge personality, intelligence, social status.
Our assessment of a person's intelligence is affected by the way he or she uses language.
linguistic factors influence how we judge personality, intelligence.
Our assessment of a person's intelligence is affected by the way he or she uses language.
linguistic factors influence how we judge personality, intelligence.
linguistic factors influence how we judge personality, intelligence.
linguistic factors influence how we judge personality, intelligence, social status.
linguistic factors influence how we judge... educational standards.
linguistic factors influence how we judge... job aptitude.
In its most general sense, prescriptivism is the view that one variety of language has an inherently higher value than others.
the favoured variety is usually a version of the "standard" written language.
Adherents to this variety are said to speak or write "correctly".
deviations from it are said to be "incorrect".
the formal spoken language which most closely reflects this style.
they wanted to codify the principles of their languages.
to show that there was a system beneath the apparent chaos of usage.
The authoritarian nature of the approach is best characterised by its reliance on "rules" of grammar.
Some usages are "prescribed", to be learnt and followed accurately.
others are "proscribed", to be avoided.
it was the task of the grammarian not simply to record alternatives, but to pronounce judgement upon them.
The opposition between "descriptivists" and "prescriptivists" has often become extreme.
it is the task of the grammarian to describe, not prescribe.
not to attempt the impossible tasks of evaluating language variation or halting language change.
the custom of speaking is the original and only just standard of any language.
grammar should be based on popular speech.
Both descriptivists and prescriptivists have been misrepresented.
both sides painting unreal pictures of the other.
Descriptive grammarians have been presented as people who do not care about standards.
Prescriptive grammarians have been presented as blind adherents to a historical tradition.
Arguments can start as easily over minor points of usage.
The view is propounded especially in relation to grammar and vocabulary.
linguistic standards should be maintained.
evaluating language variation.
the custom of speaking is the original and only just standard.
the custom of speaking is the original and only just standard.
not to attempt the impossible tasks of evaluating language variation.
I took a short nap after lunch.
Some people take a siesta in the afternoon.
Taiwan lies near the boundary of two tectonic plates.
I suffered from jet lag after returning to Taiwan.
It was a long-haul flight from Taiwan to the United States.
I like to travel because it helps me unwind.
A quiet holiday gives me time to contemplate my future.

englishVocabulary.word: cost an arm and a leg
englishVocabulary.example: Flights during the holiday season can cost an arm and a leg.
Travelling gives me a welcome change of scenery.
Travelling abroad allows me to broaden my horizons.
We saw small cafés here and there along the street.
I travel as often as I can.
The city has beautiful architecture, not to mention its amazing food.
People should not leave rubbish in public places.
Some people litter because they do not understand the environmental consequences.
I would love to try different European cuisines.
I can reheat the soup later.
Holidays give me some time off work.

englishVocabulary.meaning: 正如經常發生的情況一樣
englishVocabulary.example: As is often the case, the answer depends on whom you ask.
It is all but impossible to predict the weather perfectly.
Research into this technology is still at a very early stage.
It cannot be denied that the climate is changing.
The planet may be getting greener rather than hotter.
Several companies, including two large manufacturers, joined the project.
Traditional businesses may have most to lose from the new policy.
Some large businesses are on the side of the sceptics.
Some experts attribute temperature changes to natural cyclical changes.
The jury is still out on the main cause of the problem.

germanVocabulary.example: Es ist sehr heiß. / Ich bin heiß.
Mir ist kalt. / Ich bin kalt.
最常見,任何時間都可以
德國北部,一整天都可使用

germanVocabulary.meaning: 回頭見!(短時間內會再見)
保護, 確保, 防護
認識 (事物), 知道(某人）
人稱的 (用來稱呼人或事物)

englishGrammar.explanation: 德文的假設語氣(Konjunktiv II)可用於表達客氣、假定語氣或可能性。
在一個句子中如果出現兩個動詞，第二個動詞通常會以原型形式出現在句子的最後。

englishGrammar.rule: 動詞過去分詞與形容詞
englishGrammar.explanation: 德文的動詞過去分詞 (Partizip II) 與形容詞有時形式相似但用法不同。例如，'sich erkälten' 的過去分詞用於完成式 ('Ich habe mich erkältet' - 我感冒了)，而 'erkältet' 也可以作為形容詞，意為『感冒的』 ('Ich bin erkältet' - 我感冒了，強調狀態)。
`Es freut mich` 是一個非人稱句型，意為『我很高興』。其中 `Es` 是虛主詞，`mich` 是第一人稱的受格形式，表示『使我高興』。此句型常用於表達因某事而感到高興。

在德文中，不定詞句型 (Infinitiv mit zu) 中，`zu` 通常放在動詞原型之前。如果動詞是可分動詞 (trennbare Verben)，`zu` 會插入在前綴和動詞詞幹之間 (例如：`aufstehen` → `aufzustehen`)；如果動詞是不可分動詞 (untrennbare Verben) 或沒有前綴，`zu` 會放在整個動詞之前 (例如：`kennenlernen` → `kennenzulernen`)。
在德文的現在式句型中，可分前綴動詞的動詞詞幹會置於句子的第二位，而其前綴會移到句子的最後。

englishGrammar.rule: 德文表達身體感受的句型
englishGrammar.wrong: Ich bin heiß.
englishGrammar.correct: Mir ist heiß.
englishGrammar.explanation: 德文表達身體感受（如冷、熱）時，通常使用「Mir ist...」句型，主詞為非人稱 Es，感受由人體「承受」。若使用「Ich bin...」則會形容人本身的特質（例如「我很辣」）。

englishGrammar.rule: 理解轉折信號詞 (but, however) 表達對比與轉折
englishGrammar.wrong: Zoos in the WZCS select list were carefully inspected.
englishGrammar.correct: Zoos in the WZCS select list were NOT carefully inspected.
englishGrammar.explanation: 轉折詞 'but' 表示實際情況與預期相反。句子前半段提出一種假設，但 'but' 之後的內容揭示了事實並非如此，因此前半段的判斷是錯誤的。

englishGrammar.rule: 理解讓步信號詞 (nevertheless, although) 表達讓步與重點
englishGrammar.wrong: The fall in profits was not disappointing because it was not unexpected.
englishGrammar.correct: Although the fall in profits was not unexpected, it is nevertheless very disappointing.
englishGrammar.explanation: 讓步詞 'nevertheless' 表示儘管某事不令人意外，但其結果仍然令人失望，強調後者的重要性。

englishGrammar.rule: 理解動詞詞組 'bring about' 表示因果關係
englishGrammar.wrong: Increased air temperature merely coincided with higher sea levels.
englishGrammar.correct: Increased air temperature brought about higher sea levels (i.e., caused higher sea levels).
englishGrammar.explanation: 'bring about' 是明確表示「導致」、「造成」因果關係的動詞詞組，而非僅僅是伴隨發生。

englishGrammar.wrong: tieing
englishGrammar.correct: tying
englishGrammar.explanation: 以-ie結尾的動詞，變為現在分詞（-ing形式）時，應將-ie改為-y再加-ing。
動詞形式需根據其前方主詞的單數或複數形式來決定。

englishGrammar.correct: 某人 and 某人 do something
englishGrammar.explanation: 當主詞為複數形式（如'某人 and 某人'）時，在現在簡單式中動詞應使用原型（base form）。

englishGrammar.wrong: I drink a sip of water.
englishGrammar.correct: I took a sip of water.
englishGrammar.explanation: 描述剛完成動作應使用過去式，且搭配詞為 take a sip。

englishGrammar.wrong: Outside has a big rainy.
englishGrammar.correct: It is raining heavily outside.
englishGrammar.explanation: Rainy 是形容詞，不能當名詞。描述下雨應使用 It is raining + 副詞。

englishGrammar.wrong: Where are you stay?
englishGrammar.correct: Where are you staying?
englishGrammar.explanation: 詢問目前居住狀態應使用 be + V-ing。

englishGrammar.wrong: We have many strong earthquake every year.
englishGrammar.correct: We have many strong earthquakes every year.
englishGrammar.explanation: Many 後面需接可數名詞複數。

englishGrammar.wrong: Today is weekend.
englishGrammar.correct: Today is the weekend.
englishGrammar.explanation: 表示本週週末時需加上定冠詞 the。

englishGrammar.wrong: I buy something, and it is very expensive.
englishGrammar.correct: I bought something, and it was very expensive.
englishGrammar.explanation: 描述過去發生的事，整句時態應一致。

englishGrammar.wrong: This one is more better.
englishGrammar.correct: This one is better.
englishGrammar.explanation: Better 本身即為比較級，前面不可加 more。

englishGrammar.wrong: I decide to have the exam in November 21.
englishGrammar.correct: I decided to take the exam on November 21.
englishGrammar.explanation: 考試用 take，特定日期前介系詞用 on。

englishGrammar.wrong: I maybe have four months to prepare.
englishGrammar.correct: I may have four months to prepare.
englishGrammar.explanation: Maybe 是副詞，May 才是情態動詞，可接原形動詞。

englishGrammar.wrong: Taiwan is have many typhoons.
englishGrammar.correct: Taiwan has many typhoons.
englishGrammar.explanation: 一般現在式直接用動詞即可，不需要 be 動詞。

englishGrammar.wrong: Everywhere has a lot of water.
englishGrammar.correct: There is water everywhere.
englishGrammar.explanation: 表示某處存在某物時，使用 there be 句型更自然。

englishGrammar.wrong: I spent twelve hours to fly to Seattle.
englishGrammar.correct: I spent twelve hours flying to Seattle.
englishGrammar.explanation: Spend time 後面應接 V-ing。

englishGrammar.wrong: I felt very tired.
englishGrammar.correct: The flight was very tiring, so I felt tired.
englishGrammar.explanation: 人感到的狀態用 -ed，造成狀態的事物用 -ing。

englishGrammar.wrong: Why do you laughing?
englishGrammar.correct: Why are you laughing?
englishGrammar.explanation: 正在笑是當下發生的動作，問句結構為 be＋主詞＋V-ing。

englishGrammar.rule: 事實描述用現在簡單式
englishGrammar.wrong: The River Nile is flowing into the Mediterranean.
englishGrammar.correct: The River Nile flows into the Mediterranean.
englishGrammar.explanation: 描述河流流向這項長期事實，使用現在簡單式。

englishGrammar.rule: 暫時狀況用現在進行式
englishGrammar.wrong: The river flows very fast today.
englishGrammar.correct: The river is flowing very fast today.
englishGrammar.explanation: 句子描述今天的特殊情況，而不是河流平常的狀態。

englishGrammar.wrong: More and more people use electric cars.
englishGrammar.correct: More and more people are using electric cars.
englishGrammar.explanation: more and more 表示正在增加，強調持續發展的趨勢。

englishGrammar.wrong: What does he went?
englishGrammar.correct: What does he want?
englishGrammar.explanation: does 後面必須使用動詞原形。

englishGrammar.wrong: I’m not believing it.
englishGrammar.correct: I don’t believe it.
englishGrammar.explanation: believe 表示內心相信的狀態，通常不用現在進行式。

englishGrammar.rule: be 與 be being 的區別
englishGrammar.wrong: Are you being hungry?
englishGrammar.correct: Are you hungry?
englishGrammar.explanation: hungry 是身體狀態，不能使用 being。

germanGrammar.example: Nominativ: der Mann; Akkusativ: den Mann; Dativ: dem Mann; Genitiv: des Mannes
germanGrammar.explanation:
德語定冠詞會根據名詞的性別、單複數以及在句子中的格位（Nominativ主格、Akkusativ受格、Dativ間接受格、Genitiv所有格）而變化。
變格表：
Nominativ: der, die, das, die (plural)
Akkusativ: den, die, das, die (plural)
Dativ: dem, der, dem, den (plural)
Genitiv: des, der, des, der (plural)

germanGrammar.rule: 介系詞 in 的用法 (表示位置或方向)
germanGrammar.example: Ich gehe in das Haus. / Ich wohne in dem Haus.
germanGrammar.explanation: 當介系詞 in 表示「在哪裡」（靜態位置）時，後面接 Dativ；當 in 表示「到哪裡」（動態方向）時，後面接 Akkusativ。

germanGrammar.rule: 介系詞 zu 總是接 Dativ
germanGrammar.explanation: 介系詞 zu 常與人名、機構或地點（通常是朝向某人或某地）連用，表示去...，其後名詞一律使用 Dativ (第三格)。

germanGrammar.example: zu dem = zum; zu der = zur; in das = ins
germanGrammar.explanation: 某些介系詞與定冠詞可以縮寫成一個詞，形成慣用語。

germanGrammar.example: fahren (du fährst, er fährt); geben (du gibst, er gibt)
germanGrammar.explanation: 部分動詞在 du 和 er/sie/es 人稱時，詞幹母音會發生變化，如 a 變 ä (fahren, schlafen, laufen, tragen, halten)，e 變 i (geben, essen) 或 ie (sehen)。

germanGrammar.rule: 德語規則動詞詞尾變化
germanGrammar.example: ich fahre; du fährst; er/sie/es fährt; wir fahren; ihr fahrt; sie/Sie fahren
germanGrammar.explanation: 德語規則動詞的詞尾變化遵循固定模式：ich (-e), du (-st), er/sie/es (-t), wir (-en), ihr (-t), sie/Sie (-en)。

germanGrammar.rule: 不規則動詞 sein 的變位
germanGrammar.example: ich bin; du bist; er/sie/es ist; wir sind; ihr seid; sie/Sie sind
germanGrammar.explanation: 動詞 sein (是) 是不規則動詞，其變位需要特別記憶。
在自我介紹職稱時（如 Ich bin Student），以及表示種類或一般性不可數名詞時，通常省略冠詞。

germanGrammar.rule: 不定冠詞後形容詞的詞尾變化 (陰性 Akkusativ)
germanGrammar.explanation: 當不定冠詞後面接陰性名詞，且該名詞處於 Akkusativ (受格) 時，形容詞詞尾會加上 -e。

germanGrammar.example: Ich gehe mit meiner Mutter Gemüse kaufen.
germanGrammar.explanation: 在德語不定詞片語中，通常將受詞放在不定詞前面，即「先寫名詞(受詞)再寫動詞」。

germanGrammar.rule: 句子中人與物的格位分配 (間接受詞與直接受詞)
germanGrammar.example: Ich gehe mit meiner Mutter Gemüse kaufen.
germanGrammar.explanation: 當一個句子中同時涉及人（通常為間接受詞）和事物（通常為直接受詞）時，人通常用 Dativ (第三格)，事物用 Akkusativ (第四格)。例如 `mit meiner Mutter` (Dativ) 和 `Gemüse` (Akkusativ)。

germanGrammar.rule: 前往國家的介系詞用法
germanGrammar.example: Wir fahren nach Österreich. / Wir fahren in die Schweiz.
germanGrammar.explanation: 前往沒有定冠詞的國家使用 nach + Dativ (例如 Österreich)；前往有定冠詞的國家使用 in + Akkusativ (例如 die Schweiz)。

germanSentences.german: Nachdem er nach Taipeh gezogen ist, haben wir weniger Kontakt.
germanSentences.chinese: 在他搬到台北之後, 我們比較少聯絡了.

germanSentences.german: Die Firma schickt ihn in den Ruhestand.

germanSentences.chinese: 台北是一個有名的城市。

germanSentences.german: Ich gehe mit meiner Mutter Gemüse kaufen.

wordRoots.meaning: 球莖；膨脹物；圓形突起物
wordRoots.origin: 源自拉丁文 bulbus (意指「洋蔥，球莖」)，而 bulbus 又來自希臘文 bolbos (同樣指「洋蔥，球莖」)。這些詞語被認為最終源於原始印歐語詞根 *bhel- (意指「吹氣，膨脹」)。

examples.meaning: 這個字在英文中本身就是一個不可再分的字根，源自拉丁文「洋蔥」或「膨脹物」的意思。它不包含常見的英文前綴或後綴。
球莖狀的；膨脹的；圓胖的
球；球狀物 (與 bulb 最終共享「膨脹」的印歐語根)
氣球；熱氣球 (與 bulb 最終共享「膨脹」的印歐語根)

wordRoots.ieltsTip: 「Bulb」及其相關概念（如「膨脹」、「圓形」）常用於描述科學、地理或技術文章中的物體形狀或構造，例如在生物學中描述植物部分 (plant bulbs)、在物理學中描述燈泡 (light bulbs)。理解其「膨脹、圓形」的核心意義，有助於理解相關描述性詞彙，這在雅思閱讀和寫作的學術文本中很常見。
這是一個在學術文章和聽力中常見的正式詞彙，了解其「禁止、宣告非法」的含義以及與 prescribe (規定、開藥) 的區別，對雅思閱讀和寫作非常重要。

speakingNotes.core: Holidays provide time off work to unwind and reset.
speakingNotes.verbs: take a trip, catch up with friends, plan a trip

speakingNotes.core: Travel helps broaden horizons and experience different cuisines.
speakingNotes.verbs: broaden horizons, try different cuisines, go sightseeing
speakingNotes.idioms: a change of scenery, broaden my horizons

speakingNotes.core: Taiwan is prone to typhoons and earthquakes; buildings are designed for safety.
Business travel can be exhausting but valuable for personal growth.
speakingNotes.nouns: immigration officer, business trip, jet lag
speakingNotes.verbs: suffer from jet lag, take long-haul flights
speakingNotes.idioms: long-haul flight, cost an arm and a leg

paraphrases.original: We are still at a very early stage in understanding weather.
paraphrases.speakingExample: Our project is still at a very early stage.
paraphrases.writingExample: The study of this phenomenon is not very developed yet.

paraphrases.original: Predicting change is all but impossible.
paraphrases.speakingExample: It's nearly impossible to find a parking space here.
paraphrases.writingExample: It is all but impossible to eradicate poverty completely.

expressions.text: the sceptics, including many big businesses
many big businesses are among the sceptics

paraphrases.original: The sceptics, including a lot of big businesses...
paraphrases.speakingExample: Many big businesses are among the sceptics regarding the new policy.
paraphrases.writingExample: The sceptics, including many big businesses, argue against the tax.
